title: Entity Color ( EntColor)
text: Entity Color ( EntColor) Determines whether the widget inherits the foreground and background color of the entity. True (default) or False . Deprecated. Use Inherit Colors instead. EntColor = " True | False " Usage Dynamic? Yes Supported in Grid? Yes Dependencies: None Description Note: The Inherit Colors property is available for more widgets than EntColor and defaults to the Windows system color. However, for compatibility reasons, when EntColor=True , it takes precedence over the Inherit Colors property. This can result in an inconsistent appearance. We therefore recommend that you use Inherit Colors rather than EntColor . When EntColor is True: The edit box inherits the color of the entity. If no color is defined for the entity, the visible color is inherited from the parent frame or a form. If no color is defined for the parent, the visible color of the parent is used, not the Windows default color for edit boxes. This is in contrast to the behavior of the Inherit Colors property. For more information, see Color Inheritance . Applies to EditBox Related Links Index Color Code that identifies a Uniface index color, which represents a combination of background and foreground colors. Applicable only to Form components. Deprecated. Color Inheritance It is possible to explicitly define background and foreground colors for most objects. If you do not explicitly define a color, the actual color used depends on the type of object, and on the Inherit Colors and Transparency properties (where applicable). Inherit Colors (InheritColors) Determines whether the background and foreground colors of the parent entity or form can be inherited. This property can be set in ProcScript for the widgets, but not for labels. Valid Values are True or False (default). Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
